protocols.title: Field sample handling when cold chain is uncertain
steps: Stop movement and label the container before changing conditions.
Record current time, approximate temperature, container type, and visible leakage.
Keep the sample shaded and insulated; do not freeze unless the protocol explicitly requires freezing.
If leakage, unknown preservative, or exposure occurred, isolate the container and request supervisor review.

protocols.title: Basic non-spill decontamination checkpoint
steps: Confirm gloves are intact and remove loose materials from the work surface.
Use the locally approved disinfectant contact time from the site protocol.
Work from clean areas toward dirty areas and avoid aerosol generation.
Bag waste according to the field kit label and log completion.

firstActions: Ventilate if safe to do so.
For eye exposure, rinse with clean water and follow site emergency protocol.
For large spills or symptoms, stop work and call the site safety lead.

hazards: toxic gas if mixed with acids or ammonia
firstActions: Do not mix with acids, ammonia, or unknown cleaners.
Increase ventilation if odor is strong and safe to do so.
For skin exposure, rinse with water and remove contaminated clothing.
For breathing difficulty, leave the area and activate the site emergency plan.

hardware.name: temperature logger
checks: Confirm the logger clock and run indicator.
Check battery seating and note whether the case is wet or cracked.
Compare against a second thermometer if available.
If logger stopped during sample custody, preserve the sample and flag chain-of-custody review.

hardware.name: portable centrifuge
checks: Stop the rotor before opening the lid.
Confirm tubes are balanced by mass and placed opposite each other.
Inspect rotor, lid seal, and tube caps for cracks or leakage.
If vibration persists after balancing, do not run again until inspected.

sensorGuidance.guidance: Use short, repeatable checks. A still phone should show stable gravity direction; rotation-rate spikes suggest handling or vibration. If the phone is in a pocket, treat the reading as coarse activity context, not calibrated measurement.
sensorGuidance.confidence: medium when repeated readings agree; low when phone position is unknown

sensorGuidance.guidance: Gesture and pocket readings can mark events, repeat prompts, flag emergency context, or capture activity state. Always ask where the phone is and avoid treating pocket motion as precise measurement.
sensorGuidance.confidence: medium for deliberate confirmed gestures; low for unconfirmed pocket movement

sensorGuidance.guidance: Use pressure as a relative trend. Absolute altitude needs calibration against weather and a known reference point.
sensorGuidance.confidence: medium for short-term relative change; low for absolute elevation without calibration

sensorGuidance.guidance: Use microphone data as acoustic context: loud environment, likely overlap, interruption, or machinery noise. Do not infer exact speaker count, identity, or position from a single phone without a reviewed diarization/localization pipeline.
sensorGuidance.confidence: medium for loud/quiet and voice-activity trends; low for speaker count or location

sensorGuidance.guidance: UWB needs a compatible nearby device or tag. LiDAR scene depth only exists on supported devices and should not be required in this camera-free workflow.
sensorGuidance.confidence: medium for supported UWB proximity zones; low or unavailable otherwise
